channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: With a Round of 32 spot already clinched, the U.S. takes on Turkey in the World Cup
item.description: Two U.S. wins and two Turkey losses already on the books mean the Americans have won this World Cup group no matter the outcome of Thursday's game. Still, the Americans say they're playing to win.
item.content:encoded:
Two U.S. wins and two Turkey losses already on the books mean the Americans have won this World Cup group no matter the outcome of Thursday's game. Still, the Americans say they're playing to win.

(Image credit: Richard Heathcote)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost a year after catastrophic Texas floods
item.description: Camp Mystic, the all-girls Christian Texas summer camp, where 28 people died during the catastrophic floods last year filed for bankruptcy reorganization on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: Camp Mystic, the all-girls Christian Texas summer camp, where 28 people died during the catastrophic floods last year filed for bankruptcy reorganization on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Lucio Vasquez

item.title: One year after Grand Canyon fire, an emotional re-opening
item.description: The biggest wildfire in America last year was at Grand Canyon National Park. The area that burned recently re-opened and recovery, of the land and emotions of people who loved it has begun.
item.content:encoded: The biggest wildfire in America last year was at Grand Canyon National Park. The area that burned recently re-opened and recovery, of the land and emotions of people who loved it has begun.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Skabelund

item.title: How Ukraine's immigrant community turned Philadelphia into a soccer town
item.description: Long before most Americans paid attention to soccer, Philadelphia had a thriving culture of athletes and fans shaped in large part by Ukrainian immigrants. The sport offers a rallying place for Ukrainians at a time when their home country is fighting for survival.
item.content:encoded: Long before most Americans paid attention to soccer, Philadelphia had a thriving culture of athletes and fans shaped in large part by Ukrainian immigrants. The sport offers a rallying place for Ukrainians at a time when their home country is fighting for survival.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: 5 years after the Surfside condo collapse, the toll of the tragedy remains
item.description: Surfside, Florida, is marking five years since a beachfront condominium collapsed, killing 98 people. It was one of the largest structural failures in U.S. history.
item.content:encoded:
Surfside, Florida, is marking five years since a beachfront condominium collapsed, killing 98 people. It was one of the largest structural failures in U.S. history.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza

item.title: New York primary could forecast future for Democrats. Here's what you need to know
item.description: New York's primary election highlighted a question the Democratic Party is facing: Just how progressive does it want to be? In safe seats, progressives win, but in competitive seats, moderates prevail.
item.content:encoded:
New York's primary election highlighted a question the Democratic Party is facing: Just how progressive does it want to be? In safe seats, progressives win, but in competitive seats, moderates prevail.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Does Motown's legacy endure for Detroit's younger generations?
item.description: Motown --- the music and the label -- is central to the identity of Detroiters. The music is woven together with the city's legacy. But do younger generations feel the same way?
item.content:encoded: Motown --- the music and the label -- is central to the identity of Detroiters. The music is woven together with the city's legacy. But do younger generations feel the same way?
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: With at least 6 wildfires burning, Utah officials prep for long fire season
item.description: Several wildfires have broken out in the Beehive state. One required an entire town to be evacuated for nearly a week now.
item.content:encoded: Several wildfires have broken out in the Beehive state. One required an entire town to be evacuated for nearly a week now.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Ballard

item.title: Texas adopting stricter standards for data centers that want to join its power grid
item.description: Texas has been flooded with data center requests to join its power grid. The state seeks to sort out who actually wants to do business in Texas -- and which projects are purely speculative.
item.content:encoded: Texas has been flooded with data center requests to join its power grid. The state seeks to sort out who actually wants to do business in Texas -- and which projects are purely speculative.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: Appeals court allows Trump administration expanded use of speedy deportations
item.description: A federal appeals court on Tuesday allowed the Trump administration to resume carrying out speedy deportations of undocumented migrants throughout the United States, not just near the border.
item.content:encoded:
A federal appeals court on Tuesday allowed the Trump administration to resume carrying out speedy deportations of undocumented migrants throughout the United States, not just near the border.

(Image credit: Morgan Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
